topic: Adaptation of Cherry Studio Features: RAG Embeddings, Translation, Model Services, and Floating Lookup Helper
context: The user selected 4 specific features to borrow from Cherry Studio for OMNIX Workbench. We are documenting the technical alignment and design for these features.
options_considered: Implement features with separate packages and complex plugins

Design a unified core with 4 modules: (1) RAG manager with multi-embedding support, (2) Chat-based translation interface, (3) General LLM Provider Registry, and (4) Context-aware floating select-text widget.
chosen_option: Design a unified core with 4 modules: (1) RAG manager with multi-embedding support, (2) Chat-based translation interface, (3) General LLM Provider Registry, and (4) Context-aware floating select-text widget.
reasoning: Embedding Models: Implementing a standard adapter for local (Ollama) and API-based (OpenAI/SiliconFlow) embedding services ensures high flexibility for indexing codebases.
Translation: Relying on prompt engineering and structured markdown rendering achieves highly accurate side-by-side translations without relying on paid legacy translation APIs.
Model Services: Abstracting model providers into a clean registry decouples UI components from API specifics, adhering to the dependency inversion principle.
Floating Lookup Helper: A custom UI overlay triggered by text selection (or keyboard shortcuts) provides a frictionless experience for explaining or refactoring code snippets.
tradeoffs: System-wide floating lookups require OS-level accessibility hooks, whereas application-wide floating is lightweight and easy to implement. We will consult the user on this scope.
impact_scope: UI layer (floating components, translation side-by-side view)
future_risks: Potential OS-level permission blocks for system-wide text selection hook capture.
